user_message: Just came out of QBR preparation with Parnell Aerospace. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 15:45 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 12:01 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 10:29 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Parnell Aerospace: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-M trap (Outage-handling distributed across email-chat-context and ACR signals -- no firs); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-M failure mode (Outage-handling distributed across email-chat-context and ACR signals -- no firs)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-M -- Outage-handling distributed across email-chat-context and ACR signals -- no firs
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
